name: The Cincinnatus Savings & Loan Co.
description_short: Community bank in Cincinnati area offering checking, savings, mortgages, CDs, and consumer loans with branches in Cheviot, Harrison, and Northgate.
description_long:
Cincinnatus Savings & Loan Co. is a community bank headquartered in Cincinnati, Ohio, with a primary focus on serving residents and businesses in the greater Cincinnati region. The bank operates three physical branch locations: the main office in Cheviot, a second branch in Harrison, and a third in Northgate. The company is FDIC-insured, regulated under NMLS 405658, and operates as an Equal Housing Lender, indicating compliance with federal lending standards.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of personal and business banking products. On the personal side, customers can access checking and savings accounts, Cincinnatus-branded debit cards, Visa credit cards, certificates of deposit (CDs), individual retirement accounts (IRAs), and CDARS services. Their lending products include mortgages with advertised rates of 4.00% (15-year) and 4.875% (30-year), home equity lines of credit, auto loans, consumer loans, and equity lines. Business customers can access commercial lending, business checking and savings accounts, and business credit cards.

The bank differentiates itself through personalized community banking, emphasizing that customers are "friends and neighbors" and that the bank invests locally in Cincinnati-area residents and businesses. They offer both in-person branch service and online banking platforms, including a "Teller on Call" service (1-833-572-4895) for remote assistance. The website highlights competitive mortgage rates, CD rates, and an "Affordable Rate Program" as selling points. The bank provides online tools including a mortgage calculator and a savings/investment calculator.

As a community bank, Cincinnatus is positioned for customers seeking local, personalized service rather than national megabank convenience. However, the bank's geographic presence is limited to the Cincinnati metropolitan area, making it inaccessible to out-of-state customers seeking local branch access. The website content focuses heavily on marketing messaging rather than detailed product specifications, fee schedules, or current rate transparency, which may require customers to contact the bank directly for specific terms. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with CD specials
CDARS (Certificate of Deposit Account Registry Service)
pros: FDIC-insured member bank with federal compliance (NMLS 405658, Equal Housing Lender status)
Three physical branch locations in Cincinnati area (Cheviot, Harrison, Northgate) for in-person service
Remote banking support via 'Teller on Call' (1-833-572-4895) and online banking platform
Advertised mortgage rates at 4.00% (15-year) and 4.875% (30-year) positioned as competitive
Full-service lending including mortgages, home equity lines of credit, auto loans, and consumer loans
Business banking products including commercial lending, business checking/savings, and business credit cards
CD specials, IRA accounts, and CDARS services for savings and investment products
Online tools including mortgage calculator and savings/investment calculator for customer planning
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to Cincinnati area (Cheviot, Harrison, Northgate only) — no national branch access
Website lacks specific current rates, fee schedules, and detailed product terms; customers must contact bank directly for pricing
No mention of mobile banking app or modern digital-only account opening; unclear technology capabilities
Marketing-heavy website content provides limited substantive information about loan terms, APRs, minimum balances, or service fees
No information about business hours, appointment scheduling, or specific eligibility requirements for products
best_for: Cincinnati-area residents and small business owners seeking personalized community banking with local branch access
Homebuyers in Ohio looking for mortgage financing from a locally-invested lender
Customers who prefer in-person banking relationships and community-focused institutions over national chains
Small businesses needing comprehensive commercial lending and business banking services in the Cincinnati region
diagnosis: Cincinnatus Savings & Loan Co. is best for Cincinnati-area residents and small businesses seeking community-focused banking with personalized service and local branch access. The main caveat is geographic limitation — customers outside the Cincinnati metropolitan area cannot access physical branches, and the website provides insufficient rate and fee transparency, requiring direct contact for specific product terms and pricing.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
